title: The Nations Best
summary: A royal esports rebrand for TNB, built around a lion mascot, crown details, and a stronger clan identity.
description: A complete esports identity for The Nations Best, turning the team's royal, competitive energy into a lion mascot system with alternate marks, apparel direction, and a sharper visual presence.

sections.overview: The Nations Best needed a rebrand that felt bigger than a single mascot logo. The direction moved toward a lion identity with royal cues, giving the clan a mark that could represent strength, hierarchy, and the idea of a true competitive nation.
sections.challenge: TNB sits inside the NBA2K gaming community with players, creators, designers, and a known leadership presence behind it. The old Bengal Tiger direction no longer carried the scale of the group, so the identity needed to feel more established while still staying aggressive enough for esports culture.
sections.solution: The final direction centers on a crowned lion with bold facial structure, heavy hair shapes, sharp eyes, and castle-inspired details. Every piece was shaped to feel majestic without losing the intensity expected from a competitive gaming clan.
sections.system: The identity includes a primary mascot, alternate marks, typography direction, and supporting brand elements that can flex across profile graphics, team assets, and merch.
sections.applications: The system was designed to work across social graphics, apparel, merchandise, team presentation, and digital brand moments where the mark needs to read quickly.
sections.results: The rebrand gives TNB a stronger symbol and a more ownable presence, turning the clan identity into something that feels organized, powerful, and built around a clear visual idea.

title: DFDC Lettermark Logofolio
summary: A freestyle lettermark series created for the DFDC2 contest, built as a high-value set of custom marks.
description: A collection of custom lettermarks created for Dedicated Forever's DFDC2 contest, exploring bold initials, personal marks, and simplified logo systems for members of the community.

sections.overview: DFDC Lettermark Logofolio collects a series of freestyle marks created for the Dedicated Forever design contest. The project focuses on expressive lettermark design with enough polish to feel useful beyond a one-off contest entry.
sections.challenge: The contest theme gave wide creative freedom, so the challenge was not just making one strong graphic. The opportunity was to create something with real value for the community and give multiple members a simplified mark they could actually use.
sections.solution: Instead of treating the contest as a single-poster submission, the work became a set of alternate lettermarks for several members. Each mark was built around initials, personality, and a clean read at small sizes.
sections.system: The series explores angular initials, badge-like compositions, aggressive cuts, and simplified mascot-adjacent shapes while keeping every mark distinct.
sections.applications: The marks are suited for profile graphics, stream branding, social avatars, apparel details, and simplified companion logos for personal identities.
sections.results: The entry won by delivering more than a visual flex. It became a practical logofolio that showed range, speed, and value for the people inside the contest community.

title: AZN Draco Font
summary: A bold racing and sports display font shaped with speed, sharp corners, and aggressive forward motion.
description: AZN Draco is a racing-inspired display typeface built for high-impact logos, stream graphics, vehicle concepts, apparel headers, and sports visuals that need speed at first glance.
services: Sharp rounded corners (Not just straight rounded, but I adjusted the curve to match with the whole "fast" feel)

sections.overview: AZN Draco is a bold display font built around speed. Its italic stance, sharp serif tails, and multi-corner construction give the typeface a fast, mechanical feel without making it too complex to use.
sections.challenge: The font needed to feel aggressive and unique while still working as a usable display system. The key was balancing custom edge details with enough consistency for words, numbers, and logo-style layouts.
sections.solution: The letterforms use a single-style italic rhythm, sharp rounded corners, angled cuts, and pointed terminals. Those details create movement while keeping the alphabet readable in large headers and brand graphics.
sections.system: The typeface includes uppercase letters, numbers, and punctuation, with a visual language built from speed cues, hard corners, and sporty display proportions.
sections.applications: Draco is made for racing identities, vehicle wrap concepts, stream packages, esport headers, apparel graphics, posters, and any layout that needs a fast first impression.
sections.results: The final font feels sharp, modern, and ready for motion-heavy visuals, giving designers a display type option that carries speed without needing extra effects.

title: AZN Unified Font
summary: A sharp display font collection pairing sans and serif styles into one unified athletic type system.
description: AZN Unified is a display font collection built to connect sharp sans and serif styles, giving brand, sports, and apparel layouts a flexible but consistent typographic voice.

sections.overview: AZN Unified was created as a display font collection where sans and serif styles can live together without feeling like separate worlds. The goal was to build a type family with a shared attitude across different headline moods.
sections.challenge: Sans and serif display fonts often pull a layout in different directions. This project needed both styles to feel related, so the sharpness, width, rhythm, and overall energy had to stay consistent.
sections.solution: The collection uses strong vertical forms, pointed cuts, and athletic proportions to connect the styles. The sans direction keeps things direct and modern, while the serif direction adds a more classic display edge.
sections.system: The type system is built around unified proportions, sharp terminals, and coordinated character details so the styles can be mixed inside one identity or campaign.
sections.applications: Unified is suited for team graphics, apparel drops, poster headlines, brand lockups, esports layouts, and any project that needs both modern and classic display options.
sections.results: The collection gives designers a flexible typographic toolkit with one consistent personality, making it easier to build layouts that feel varied but still connected.

title: AZN Knuckles Font
summary: A modern slab serif varsity font built for athletic graphics, bold headlines, and collegiate-style apparel.
description: AZN Knuckles modernizes the slab serif varsity look with heavier attitude, clean display proportions, and a sports-ready feel for apparel, posters, logos, and team graphics.

sections.overview: AZN Knuckles is a modern slab serif display font inspired by varsity and athletic lettering. It keeps the familiar strength of collegiate type while pushing the shapes toward a cleaner, more contemporary sports look.
sections.challenge: Varsity-inspired fonts can easily feel generic or overly traditional. The challenge was to keep the athletic heritage while making the font feel stronger, sharper, and more usable for current brand and apparel work.
sections.solution: The forms use bold slab construction, compact proportions, and confident spacing to create a sturdy headline voice. The details are simplified enough for practical use but still carry the weight and character of sports lettering.
sections.system: The typeface is built as a display system for large words, short phrases, lockups, and merch graphics where impact matters more than long-form reading.
sections.applications: Knuckles works for varsity apparel, team graphics, mascot lockups, championship posters, gym banners, patches, and bold identity headlines.
sections.results: The finished typeface gives the classic varsity mood a modern backbone, making it feel tough, readable, and ready for athletic brand applications.

title: Quick Showcase
summary: A curated visual archive of mascot logos, gaming identities, and quick brand explorations.
description: A curated visual archive collecting mascot logos, gaming identities, and fast-moving brand explorations created across client work, community projects, and quick visual studies.

sections.overview: Quick Showcase is built as a visual archive instead of a traditional case study. It gathers selected mascot logos, gaming marks, and identity explorations into one long-form scroll so the work can be viewed for shape, attitude, style range, and execution.
sections.challenge: Because these pieces come from different contexts, the page keeps the explanation light and lets the images carry the rhythm. The goal is to make the collection feel curated and easy to browse without forcing every mark into a single project narrative.
sections.solution: The showcase is arranged as a direct image-led sequence, giving each piece enough room to breathe while keeping the page moving like a portfolio reel.
sections.system: The collection spans mascot marks, esports identities, personal brands, gaming graphics, and logo explorations.
sections.applications: The work is presented as a visual reference for style, personality, and mark-making across multiple identity directions.
sections.results: A focused archive that shows range, consistency, and energy across years of selected design work.
